Why AASIOM

Built for India's Sovereign Future

Three foundational principles define how we build, what we protect, and why institutions can trust AASIOM.

⌂

Sovereignty First

Every system is designed from first principles — hardware, firmware, software — with zero foreign dependencies as the direction.

Strategic Principle
▦

Full-Stack Indigenous

From custom SoC architectures to edge neural networks to encrypted mesh comms, AASIOM works to control every layer of the stack.

Engineering Depth
◈

IP-First Architecture

Every novel design, algorithm, and architecture is assessed for protection before it is shared. Indian IP remains in India.

Strategic Posture

India Is Not a Market.
India Is the Mission.

AASIOM was founded on the belief that India's sovereign capability gap — in autonomous systems, edge intelligence, communications, and indigenous semiconductors — is a national-security challenge the private sector must help close.

We design systems India's government can deploy, own, and operate without foreign licence dependencies.
We build for public-sector scale — infrastructure, transport, defence, and strategic national capability.
Every rupee of R&D should strengthen India's permanent technical capability, not vendor lock-in.
Technology Domains

Three Horizons of Deep Technology

AASIOM's R&D spans interconnected domains — each a strategic national capability, each designed with an indigenous-first approach.

NOW

Drone Systems

Autonomous VTOL and fixed-wing UAV platforms for critical operational environments.

Autonomous Systems
NOW

Edge AI

On-device inference and decision support without cloud dependency in the field.

Intelligence Layer
NEXT

Mesh Comms

Encrypted, resilient communication networks for corridors, patrols, and remote assets.

Connectivity
FUTURE

Semiconductors

Custom SoC design and indigenous silicon capability for India's long-term technology independence.

Silicon Future
Flagship System

ANVIRA — Our Flagship System

AASIOM's first sovereign autonomous-surveillance system, planned for Maharashtra expressway corridors as the MH-ASLN pilot.

Autonomous aerial patrol for high-risk highway corridors.
On-device incident detection designed for rapid operational awareness.
Resilient mesh relay concept for field connectivity and response coordination.
Status: in pilot planning — 2026.
Explore ANVIRA
CONCEPT / PILOT
Trust Posture

Why Institutions Can Build With AASIOM

AASIOM is structured for serious engagement: indigenous stack discipline, IP-first thinking, and honest execution over announcements.

No Foreign Dependencies by Design

Systems are planned around an indigenous-first architecture.

Protect Before You Share

Novel designs and architectures are evaluated for IP protection first.

Government as End User

We build for public infrastructure, defence-grade seriousness, and operational trust.

Execution Over Announcements

We stay honest about stage, progress, and what is still being built.
